body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
A persistent Story Library is foundational, but we can stand out by making re-entry friction near zero (Quick Resume, clean archive model, clear empty states) instead of just matching generic file lists. This supports habit-building better than feature-heavy but noisier dashboards.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer positions manuscript/project organization as a core product value (book/chapter workflow and project-centric writing): https://livingwriter.com/features
- Campfire emphasizes broad modules/worldbuilding systems, which can add complexity for writers who primarily need fast manuscript continuation: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt’s writer experience is oriented toward platform publishing/community loops as much as drafting workflow, so private draft management depth is an opportunity for us: https://writer.inkitt.com

## Risks / trade-offs
- This can become pure parity if we only ship CRUD without stronger resume flow and information scent.
- Library architecture decisions now can constrain later features (search, tags, collaboration).
- Archive/delete UX must prevent accidental loss to preserve trust.

## Recommendation
Approve. Keep scope, but require design to prioritize `resume-first` hierarchy (Recent + Quick Resume above management controls) and low-clutter navigation so the library feels faster than competitor dashboards.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Market Research Review Request

Please approve, filter, or reframe this ticket using differentiation against Living Writer/Campfire and writer-first workflow clarity.

- Source goal: Write a story writing app
- Requested output: decision + differentiation note + any scope adjustments before engineering
